input: My order #44219 still has not shipped after 6 business days. Can you check the status?
expected: I see order #44219 was placed on the 14th. It is currently at our fulfillment center awaiting carrier pickup, scheduled to ship within 24 hours. You will receive a tracking number by email once it is in transit.

input: Hi, I was charged twice for the same subscription this month. Please refund the duplicate.
expected: I see two charges of $24.99 on the 3rd, both for the Pro plan. I have refunded the duplicate; the credit should appear on your statement within 5 to 7 business days. Apologies for the inconvenience.

input: The promo code SUMMER20 is not working at checkout. It says invalid.
expected: SUMMER20 expired on May 31. Our current promotion is SPRING15 for 15% off your first order. I have applied it to your cart; please refresh and complete checkout.

input: How do I cancel my subscription? I cannot find the button in my account.
expected: You can cancel from Account > Billing > Manage Plan > Cancel Subscription. If the button is hidden, you may be on an annual contract; reply with your account email and I can cancel it for you directly.

input: The package arrived but item SKU-7782 is missing from the box. The other two items are there.
expected: Apologies for the missing item. I have shipped a replacement for SKU-7782 via priority mail (tracking will arrive by email within an hour). No return needed for the original shipment.

input: I want to change the delivery address on order #51008. It has not shipped yet.
expected: Order #51008 is still in pre-shipment. I have updated the delivery address to the new one on file. You will receive a confirmation email within 10 minutes; please reply if anything looks wrong.

input: Your app keeps logging me out every few minutes on my Android. It is unusable.
expected: This is a known issue in app version 4.12.1 on Android 14. Please update to 4.12.3 from the Play Store; the fix is in that release. If the problem persists after updating, share the device model and we will dig deeper.

input: Can you tell me the return window on holiday purchases?
expected: Holiday purchases (made between November 1 and December 31) are returnable through January 31 of the following year. Standard 30-day return rules apply outside that window.

input: I never received the verification email after signup. I checked spam too.
expected: Verification emails from no-reply@example.com sometimes fail with strict corporate filters. I have manually verified your account. Try logging in now; if you still cannot, reply with a non-corporate email and I will re-issue.

input: Do you offer student discounts? I am a verified university student.
expected: Yes, we offer 25% off the Plus plan for verified students through SheerID. Visit /students to verify your status; the discount applies automatically at checkout once verified.

input: My credit card got declined but it works everywhere else. What is going on?
expected: Decline codes from this card issuer often indicate a billing-address mismatch. Please confirm the address on file matches the statement address. If they match, contact your bank to authorize the charge from our merchant ID and retry.

input: I need to update the credit card on my account to a new one.
expected: Go to Account > Billing > Payment Methods > Add New Card. Set the new card as default, then remove the old one. The next billing cycle will charge the new card automatically.

input: How do I export all of my data before I close my account?
expected: From Account > Privacy > Export My Data, request an export. We will email you a download link within 24 hours. The archive includes profile, orders, messages, and uploaded files in JSON plus original formats.

input: The site keeps showing a 502 error when I try to check out. Tried two browsers.
expected: A 502 at checkout usually points to a payment-gateway timeout on our side. We are seeing elevated latency at the moment; status is at /status. Please retry in 10 minutes; if it persists, share the order amount and I can place the order manually.

input: I want to upgrade from Basic to Pro. Will I be charged the difference today?
expected: Yes. Upgrading mid-cycle pro-rates the price difference and charges it immediately; access to Pro features unlocks within seconds. Your renewal date stays the same.

input: Classify the sentiment of this review: 'The product broke after one use. Completely useless.'
expected: negative

input: Classify the sentiment of this review: 'Works exactly as described, shipped fast, very happy.'
expected: positive

input: Classify the sentiment of this review: 'It does what it claims. Average build quality.'
expected: neutral

input: Classify the intent of this message: 'Where is my order?'
expected: order_status

input: Classify the intent of this message: 'I want my money back.'
expected: refund_request

input: Classify the intent of this message: 'How do I reset my password?'
expected: account_help

input: Classify the support ticket priority: 'Production database is down, no customers can log in.'
expected: P0

input: Classify the support ticket priority: 'A button is misaligned on the settings page.'
expected: P3

input: Classify the support ticket priority: 'Payment processing is failing for 1 in 10 transactions.'
expected: P1

input: Is this email spam or legitimate? 'CONGRATULATIONS!!! You have WON a $1000 gift card. Click here NOW to claim.'
expected: spam

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The quarterly revenue grew 18% year over year, driven primarily by expansion in the European market where customer count doubled. North American growth was flat, and APAC contracted 4% on currency headwinds. Operating margin improved 2 points to 24%.'
expected: Quarterly revenue grew 18% YoY led by Europe doubling its customer base, while North America was flat and APAC contracted 4%; operating margin improved to 24%.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'Researchers at MIT demonstrated a new battery chemistry using sodium and aluminum that achieves 80% of lithium-ion energy density at one third the cost, with no rare earth metals.'
expected: MIT researchers unveiled a sodium-aluminum battery hitting 80% of lithium-ion energy density at a third of the cost without rare earth metals.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The court ruled 5 to 4 that the state law preempted the federal regulation in cases of overlapping jurisdiction, reversing the appellate decision. The dissent argued the ruling undermines a century of precedent.'
expected: By a 5-4 vote the court held the state law preempts the federal regulation in overlapping jurisdictions, reversing the appellate ruling over a dissent warning of upended precedent.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'Hurricane Lyra made landfall as a Category 3 storm at 2 a.m. local time, knocking out power to 1.8 million homes and causing widespread flooding along the coast. Two deaths have been reported, and shelters across three counties are at capacity.'
expected: Hurricane Lyra hit as a Cat 3 at 2 a.m., killing two, cutting power to 1.8 million homes, flooding the coast, and filling shelters across three counties.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The committee voted unanimously to advance the bill out of markup with 14 amendments incorporated, including a sunset clause requiring reauthorization in 2030 and a provision for annual GAO audits.'
expected: The committee voted unanimously to send the bill onward with 14 amendments, including a 2030 sunset and annual GAO audits.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'Wildfire conditions across the western counties have escalated to extreme due to a heat dome and 35 mph dry winds; the governor declared a state of emergency and pre-positioned 1,200 firefighters.'
expected: Extreme wildfire conditions from a heat dome and 35 mph winds prompted the governor to declare an emergency and pre-position 1,200 firefighters in the western counties.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The merger creates the second largest player in the regional grocery market by store count, but the FTC will scrutinize the combined entity's share in three metros where overlap exceeds 40%.'
expected: The merger forms the region's second largest grocer by store count and will draw FTC scrutiny in three metros where the combined share tops 40%.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The athlete broke the world record in the 400m hurdles by 0.18 seconds, finishing in 45.94, and credited a new training block focused on lactate threshold work.'
expected: The athlete shattered the 400m hurdles world record by 0.18 seconds to 45.94, crediting a lactate-threshold training block.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'Inflation cooled to 2.4% year over year in April, down from 3.1% in March, led by declines in energy and used vehicles; shelter inflation remained sticky at 5.2%.'
expected: April inflation eased to 2.4% YoY from 3.1% on falling energy and used-vehicle prices, though shelter stayed sticky at 5.2%.

input: Summarize in one sentence: 'The vaccine showed 92% efficacy in the phase 3 trial of 28,000 participants and was well tolerated, with the most common side effect being fatigue lasting under 48 hours.'
expected: The vaccine hit 92% efficacy in a 28,000-person phase 3 trial with fatigue under 48 hours as the main side effect.

input: Extract the customer name, order number, and total from: 'Hi, this is Marcus Chen, my order 88421 came to $134.50.'
expected: {"customer_name":"Marcus Chen","order_number":"88421","total":134.50}

input: Extract the date, time, and meeting subject from: 'Quick sync about the Q3 roadmap on June 5 at 2 PM PT.'
expected: {"date":"June 5","time":"2 PM PT","subject":"Q3 roadmap"}

input: Extract first name, last name, email, and phone from: 'Sarah Patel sarah.patel@example.org (415) 555-0142'
expected: {"first_name":"Sarah","last_name":"Patel","email":"sarah.patel@example.org","phone":"(415) 555-0142"}

input: Extract the make, model, year, and mileage from: '2019 Honda Civic with 47,300 miles, one owner, well maintained.'
expected: {"make":"Honda","model":"Civic","year":2019,"mileage":47300}

input: Extract the medication, dose, and frequency from: 'Take amoxicillin 500 mg three times a day for 10 days.'
expected: {"medication":"amoxicillin","dose":"500 mg","frequency":"three times a day","duration":"10 days"}

input: Extract the airline, flight number, departure city, and arrival city from: 'United UA 845 from SFO to NRT departs at 11:50.'
expected: {"airline":"United","flight_number":"UA 845","departure_city":"SFO","arrival_city":"NRT","departure_time":"11:50"}

input: Extract the company, role, salary, and start date from: 'Joined Acme Corp as Senior Engineer at $185k starting July 1.'
expected: {"company":"Acme Corp","role":"Senior Engineer","salary":185000,"start_date":"July 1"}

input: Extract the street, city, state, and zip from: 'Ship to 1240 Mission St, Apt 4B, San Francisco, CA 94103.'
expected: {"street":"1240 Mission St, Apt 4B","city":"San Francisco","state":"CA","zip":"94103"}

input: Extract event name, venue, and date from: 'KubeCon North America is at the Salt Palace, Salt Lake City, October 8 to 11.'
expected: {"event_name":"KubeCon North America","venue":"Salt Palace","city":"Salt Lake City","start_date":"October 8","end_date":"October 11"}

input: Extract product name, price, and rating from: 'The XR-9 wireless headphones are $179 and rated 4.6 out of 5 across 2,140 reviews.'
expected: {"product_name":"XR-9 wireless headphones","price":179,"rating":4.6,"reviews":2140}

input: Write a SQL query that returns the top 5 customers by total order amount in 2025 from an orders table with columns customer_id, order_total, order_date.
expected:
SELECT customer_id, SUM(order_total) AS total_amount
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= '2025-01-01' AND order_date < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY customer_id
ORDER BY total_amount DESC
LIMIT 5;

input: Write a Bash one-liner that finds the 10 largest files under the current directory and prints their sizes in human-readable form.
expected: find . -type f -exec du -h {} + | sort -rh | head -n 10
